facts_disclosed: Grandmother raised her til 10, sends $400 CAD monthly

turns.content: ugh okay so I need to figure out the cheapest way to send money to Jamaica every month. been using Western Union but the fees are KILLING me

Western Union fees are brutal, especially for regular transfers. There are definitely cheaper options! A few questions first:

1. How much are you sending roughly each time?
2. Does the person receiving it pick up cash, or do they have a bank account?
3. How quickly does it need to get there?
I send $400 CAD every month to my grandmother. she picks up cash usually because she doesnt really do the online banking ting. and it doesnt have to be instant but like within a couple days is good

Okay, $400 CAD monthly with cash pickup in Jamaica — here are your best options ranked by cost:

1. **Wise (formerly TransferWise)** — usually the cheapest. Fees around $4-6 CAD for $400, and they give you the real exchange rate (mid-market rate) instead of marking it up. They have cash pickup options in Jamaica through partner locations.

2. **Remitly** — specifically designed for remittances. Fees vary but often $3-5 for bank transfers, slightly more for cash pickup. They have good Jamaica coverage. First transfer is usually free.

3. **WorldRemit** — similar to Remitly, good Caribbean coverage. Cash pickup available at major banks in Jamaica. Fees around $4-8 CAD.

Compare that to Western Union which probably charges you $12-15+ in fees AND marks up the exchange rate by 2-4%. On $400 monthly, you could be losing $25-30 each time.

Your grandmother — does she live near a major town where she could pick up from a bank or agent location?
she's in Kingston so yea theres plenty of pickup spots. and WOW I didnt know it was that much difference in fees!! thats like $300 a year im losing to Western Union for NO reason

Exactly — $300 a year adds up fast! That's money that could be going straight to your grandmother instead of Western Union's pocket.

For Kingston, you're in great shape. All three of those services have multiple pickup locations there:

- **Wise** partners with NCB (National Commercial Bank) and some other agents
- **Remitly** works with NCB, JN Money, and others
- **WorldRemit** has pickups at Scotiabank, NCB, and various agent locations

My recommendation: try **Remitly** first since they usually offer the first transfer free, so you can test it out with zero risk. Then compare with Wise on the second month and see which gives a better rate.

One thing to check — make sure your grandmother has valid ID for pickup. Usually a Jamaican national ID or passport works. Does she have someone who helps her with that kind of thing, or does she handle it herself?
she handles it herself still!! dont let the age fool you lol she is 78 and SHARP. raised me by herself til I was 10 and that woman is tougher than anyone I know. she'll walk to NCB no problem

Ha, love that! 78 and still handling business — that's a strong woman right there. And she raised you on her own? That's no small thing.

Since she's comfortable going to NCB, I'd go with either Wise or Remitly since both partner with NCB in Kingston. She just walks in with her ID and the pickup code you text her, and she's good.

Here's how to set it up:

1. Download Remitly (start there for the free first transfer)
2. Set up your Canadian bank account or debit card as the funding source
3. Enter your grandmother as the recipient — her full name exactly as it appears on her ID
4. Choose "cash pickup" and select NCB Jamaica
5. Send it — she'll get a text/you can call her with the reference number

You can also set up **recurring transfers** so it automatically sends every month on whatever date you choose. That way you never forget and she always gets it on time.

recurring transfer is SMART because sometimes I get caught up in the studio and forget what day it is lol. she never asks for the money either thats the ting. too proud. but I KNOW she needs it so I just make sure it goes out

That says a lot about both of you honestly — her pride and your consistency. The fact that you make it a priority every month no matter what is real love.

With recurring transfers on Remitly, you can set it to go out on the 1st of every month (or whatever day works) and it just pulls from your bank automatically. She gets the pickup notification, walks to NCB, done. You don't have to think about it even when you're deep in a session.

One more tip: **save the Remitly receipt/confirmation every month**. If you ever need to prove consistent support for immigration paperwork or tax purposes, having that trail is valuable. Remitly keeps records in-app too but screenshots don't hurt.

Also worth knowing — Canada doesn't tax money you send as gifts to family abroad, so you don't need to worry about that side of things. The $400/month is well under any reporting threshold.

oh thats good to know about the tax ting!! and yea I'll set it up for the 1st of every month. she always tells people at church that her granddaughter makes music on the computer lol she doesnt fully get what producing is but she's SO proud and it makes me wanna cry honestly

That is genuinely beautiful. She might not know the technical side of what you do, but she knows her granddaughter is out there creating something and making it work — and she's telling everybody about it. That kind of pride from someone who raised you? That hits different.

And honestly, "makes music on the computer" is a pretty accurate description of producing! She gets it more than she thinks.

So to wrap up your money transfer plan:

1. **Download Remitly** this week, do the first free transfer
2. **Set up recurring** for the 1st of each month, $400 CAD
3. **NCB Kingston** as the pickup location
4. **Save confirmations** in a folder on your phone
5. **Compare with Wise** after a month to see if the rate is better

You'll save roughly $20-25 per month compared to Western Union. Over a year that's almost $300 back in your pocket — or an extra month's worth of support for your grandmother.
an extra month of support thats a CRAZY way to look at it. okay im downloading Remitly RIGHT NOW. thank you fr fr this is gonna make the whole ting so much easier

You're welcome! It's a quick setup — probably 10 minutes and you'll be done. Make sure you have your Canadian bank info handy and your grandmother's full legal name.

And give her a call when the first one goes through on the new system so she knows to expect the NCB pickup instead of Western Union. Quick heads up so she's not confused when the process looks a little different.

Take care of yourself and your grandmother — she sounds like an amazing woman!
